competitors.label: The metadata element
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN element reference for the meta tag, its attributes, and how browsers interpret metadata in the head.
theirStrengths: MDN is the authoritative reference for the meta element and its permitted attributes and behaviors.
theirGaps: The reference format is less interview-shaped and less explicit about broken previews, indexing surprises, or viewport debugging.

realWorldUseCases: A page can look perfect in the browser and still fail in production because the head metadata is wrong
followUpConfusion: keywords is largely legacy and not the reason modern pages rank

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines the element precisely, but our answer now grounds the topic in a concrete viewport or social-preview debugging scenario.
actionableExamples: It remains the canonical tag reference, so the concrete-example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is now more explicit about legacy keywords, canonical-link confusion, and which meta tags usually matter most in practice.

actionableExamples: Keep the essential-head snippet compact so the page stays faster to scan than a full reference page.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it organizes metadata around the production failures developers actually debug: viewport, indexing, and broken social previews.
